disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Several days of heavy thirst and passing large volumes of urine in a person with known Type 2 diabetes
trigger_question_examples: How long has he been unwell?
Has he been drinking and passing urine more than usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For about a week he has been incredibly thirsty and drinking constantly, and he was passing huge amounts of urine, especially earlier in the week.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive decline in consciousness — drowsy, disoriented to time and place, does not recognise his own name
trigger_question_examples: How is he responding to you now?
Is he confused or more sleepy than normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Today he is barely rousable, he doesn't know his own name and can hardly open his eyes — he has been getting more and more drowsy over the last day or two.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced oral intake and missed diabetes medication for several days because of lethargy
trigger_question_examples: Has he been eating and drinking normally?
Has he been able to take his usual tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — he's been too flat and sleepy to drink much or take his diabetes tablets for the last few days.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No nausea, no vomiting and no abdominal pain; staff have not noticed rapid or deep breathing
trigger_question_examples: Has he been vomiting or holding his tummy?
Is his breathing fast, deep or laboured?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No vomiting and no tummy pain that we've seen, and his breathing looks normal — not fast or heavy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Facility fingerstick meter reads 'HI' — glucose above the meter's readable limit
trigger_question_examples: Have you checked his blood sugar?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We did a fingerprick and the meter just says 'HI' — it won't even give a number.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Strong, concentrated-smelling urine noted by staff over recent days
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed anything about his urine?
Any signs of a urine infection?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His urine smells very strong and concentrated, and there's a smell of it in the room.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Staff-measured low blood pressure (90/55) with a fast pulse (112) and very dry mouth with tented skin
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to take his blood pressure and pulse?
Does his skin stay pinched when you lift it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His blood pressure is low at 90 over 55 and his pulse is fast at about 112; his mouth is bone dry and the skin stays tented when I pinch it.
